task_completion.description: Open the food.gdt dataset in gretl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gretl main window is visible, showing the dataset 'food.gdt' loaded with variables including 'food_exp' and 'income'.

task_completion.description: Estimate Model A (linear) and Model B (log) to generate their fitted values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: New variables representing the fitted values from both the linear model and the log model (e.g., yhat, yhat1, yhat2, or custom names) appear in the gretl main window variable list.

task_completion.description: Run the auxiliary regression to test Model A.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A gretl model output window shows an OLS regression where the dependent variable is 'food_exp' and the independent variables include 'income' and the fitted values from Model B.

task_completion.description: Save the auxiliary regression output for Model A to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows the output being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/aux_linear.txt'.

task_completion.description: Run the auxiliary regression to test Model B.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A gretl model output window shows an OLS regression where the dependent variable is 'food_exp' and the independent variables include the log of income (e.g., 'l_income' or 'log_income') and the fitted values from Model A.

task_completion.description: Save the auxiliary regression output for Model B to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows the output being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/aux_log.txt'.

integrity.description: The agent used gretl to perform the regressions rather than faking the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine gretl GUI windows (main window, model outputs, variable creation) or valid gretl CLI (gretlcli) execution, not just text being typed into an editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the expected results into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output files are generated via gretl's save/export functionality or redirection from a gretl script, rather than the agent manually typing regression tables into a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent saved the files to the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths in the save dialogs or terminal commands exactly match '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset 'food.gdt' contains 40 observations. The correct results for the Davidson-MacKinnon J-test auxiliary regressions are: Auxiliary Linear regression (testing Model A) should have a coefficient of approximately 0.1060 and a t-statistic of 0.1490 for the fitted values. Auxiliary Log regression (testing Model B) should have a coefficient of approximately 0.9028 and a t-statistic of 1.3181 for the fitted values. Tolerances are 0.15 for coefficients and 0.5 for t-statistics. Note that any metadata claiming otherwise is incorrect.
